lesson_title: Aggressive Play: When to Double Down

introduction.text: Welcome to the 'Money Zone'. In this lesson, you will learn how to double your potential winnings. The rule is simple: When you have a strong starting hand and I have a weak card, you must attack! We use the 'Double Down' button. You double the bet, but you get ONLY one card. Let's make some money!

scenarios.title: The Holy Grail (11 vs 6)
scenarios.goal: Teach that 11 is the best hand to Double.

sequence.text: You have 11 (6+5). This is the best starting hand in Blackjack! I have a weak 6. You have a huge statistical advantage. Don't just Hit. CRUSH me. Click DOUBLE.

finish.text: BOOM! You got a King for a total of 21. I had 16 and busted. You win double the money!

scenarios.title: Strong 10 (10 vs 9)
scenarios.goal: Teach Doubling on 10 against anything weaker than 10.

sequence.text: You have 10. I have 9. It looks close, but math is on your side. With 10, you have a high chance of landing a 20. Attack! Click DOUBLE.

finish.text: Nice! You landed a 20. I had 15 and had to hit, resulting in a bust. Aggression pays off.

scenarios.title: The Sneaky 9 (9 vs 4)
scenarios.goal: Teach that 9 is only doubled against very weak dealers (3-6).

sequence.text: You have 9. Usually, we just Hit. But look at my card: it's a 4 (very weak). This is a window of opportunity. Double Down on 9 ONLY against 3, 4, 5, or 6.

finish.text: Perfect execution. You got 19. I had 12, took a Queen, and busted.

scenarios.title: Don't Get Greedy (10 vs 10)
scenarios.goal: Teach NOT to double when the dealer is strong.

sequence.text: You have 10 (5+5). Dealer has a King (10). BE CAREFUL! I am too strong. If you double and get a low card, you lose twice. Just play it safe. Click HIT.
You have 19. That is safe. Now Stand.

finish.text: Smart move. You won with 19 vs 18. If you had doubled, you would have risked too much against my strong card.

scenarios.title: Fearless 11 (11 vs Ace)
scenarios.goal: Teach that with 11 we Double even against an Ace.

sequence.text: You have 11 against my Ace. Many players are scared here. But your table says: DOUBLE! 11 is too powerful to play passively. Take the risk.

finish.text: Fortune favors the bold! You hit 21. I have 17 (Ace+6). You crushed the Ace!

conclusion.text: You are becoming a shark! You know when to attack. But what if you have a weak hand and I have a strong card? That's the hardest part of the game. We will tackle 'Stiff Hands' in the next lesson.
